messages.content: We refunded a customer their full payment. Does Razorpay waive its processing fee on that transaction?
Yes - under Part B, Part I, Clause 3.4, Razorpay waives its fees whenever you issue a full refund to the customer, so you owe nothing on that transaction.

source_clauses.display_citation: Part B SPECIFIC TERMS AND CONDITIONS, Part I Specific Terms For Payment Aggregation Services, Clause 3.4, REFUNDS
source_clauses.relevant_quote: Razorpay PA shall waive its fees on any Transaction that You have refunded in full to Your customer.

known_facts.fact: The merchant issued a full refund to the customer.

adversarial_metadata.planted_flaw: fabricated relevant_quote: the quoted waiver language does not exist in Clause 3.4 (the clause says the opposite - fees remain payable after refunds)
adversarial_metadata.expectation_notes: quote-containment check should fail because the quote is not a substring of the cited clause text

messages.content: Can Razorpay change the fees it charges us without telling us first?
Yes - Part Z, Clause 9.9 allows Razorpay to revise its fees at any time without prior notice to the merchant.

source_clauses.display_citation: Part Z IMAGINARY TERMS, Clause 9.9, FEES
source_clauses.relevant_quote: Razorpay may revise fees at any time without notice.

known_facts.fact: The merchant has an active Razorpay agreement.

adversarial_metadata.planted_flaw: citation to a clause that does not exist in the document (PartZ.9.9)
adversarial_metadata.expectation_notes: clause-id resolution should fail; coverage_metadata cannot be derived from any real cited record

messages.content: A customer reported an unauthorised card payment. Can Razorpay hold our settlements?
Razorpay can suspend settlements to you during the investigation, per Part B, Part I, Clause 4.1.

source_clauses.display_citation: Part B SPECIFIC TERMS AND CONDITIONS, Part I Specific Terms For Payment Aggregation Services, Clause 4.1, FRAUDULENT TRANSACTIONS
source_clauses.relevant_quote: Razorpay PA shall be entitled to suspend settlements to You during the pendency of inquiries, investigations and resolution thereof by the Facility Providers

known_facts.fact: A customer reported an unauthorised card transaction.

adversarial_metadata.planted_flaw: clarification_required row with empty missing_facts, clarifying_questions, and conditional_outcomes, and an assistant answer that never asks a question
adversarial_metadata.expectation_notes: category-shape validation should fail; the category-fit lint should also flag the missing clarifying question

messages.content: A customer says a payment from their card was unauthorised. Will Razorpay hold our settlement money?

It depends on where the dispute stands - can you provide more details? Under Part B, Part I, Clause 4.1, Razorpay may suspend settlements during the pendency of the investigation, but only if the Facility Provider has intimated Razorpay of the unauthorised debit.

source_clauses.display_citation: Part B SPECIFIC TERMS AND CONDITIONS, Part I Specific Terms For Payment Aggregation Services, Clause 4.1, FRAUDULENT TRANSACTIONS
source_clauses.relevant_quote: Razorpay PA shall be entitled to suspend settlements to You during the pendency of inquiries, investigations and resolution thereof by the Facility Providers

known_facts.fact: A customer reported an unauthorised card payment.

missing_facts.fact: Whether the Facility Provider has intimated Razorpay of the unauthorised debit
missing_facts.why_it_matters: Clause 4.1 only entitles Razorpay to suspend settlements once a Facility Provider has intimated it of the unauthorised debit.

conditional_outcomes.condition_summary: Facility Provider has intimated Razorpay of the unauthorised debit
conditional_outcomes.outcome: Razorpay PA may suspend settlements to you during the pendency of inquiries, investigations and resolution.

adversarial_metadata.planted_flaw: schema-valid clarification row whose clarifying question is the vague 'Can you provide more details?' instead of a targeted missing-fact question
adversarial_metadata.expectation_notes: the vague-clarifier lint should flag this; the blind judge should also penalize category_specific_behavior

messages.content: If tokenization fails under TokenHQ and customer payments are disrupted, who bears the liability?

The ToS does not resolve this: the cited material from Part B, Part III is the Preamble introducing the TokenHQ service, and no clause in that cited text allocates liability for tokenization failures or payment disruption. We recommend seeking written clarification from Razorpay before relying on any assumption about liability.

source_clauses.display_citation: Part B SPECIFIC TERMS AND CONDITIONS, Part III Specific Terms For TokenHQ Services, Preamble
source_clauses.relevant_quote: Part III: Specific Terms For TokenHQ Services

known_facts.fact: The merchant uses TokenHQ services for card tokenization.

ambiguity_reason.explanation: The cited TokenHQ preamble does not specify liability allocation for tokenization failures; the cited text is silent on this point.

adversarial_metadata.planted_flaw: schema-valid ambiguity row whose only cited support is a thin section heading (the Part III preamble) instead of substantive clause text
adversarial_metadata.expectation_notes: the thin-source and weak-ambiguity-source lints should flag this; the judge may also penalize citation_source_sufficiency

messages.content: We refunded a customer's payment in full. Do we still owe Razorpay the fee on that transaction?

The ToS is not entirely clear on whether fees remain payable after a full refund. Clause 3.4 of Part B, Part I discusses fees in the context of refunds, but we recommend confirming with Razorpay how it applies to your situation before assuming an outcome.

source_clauses.display_citation: Part B SPECIFIC TERMS AND CONDITIONS, Part I Specific Terms For Payment Aggregation Services, Clause 3.4, REFUNDS
source_clauses.relevant_quote: Razorpay PA fees shall always be applicable and payable by You on each Transaction irrespective of whether You have refunded the same to Your customer

known_facts.fact: The merchant refunded the customer's payment in full.

ambiguity_reason.explanation: The ToS does not specify clearly enough whether the fee obligation survives a full refund.

adversarial_metadata.planted_flaw: mislabeled row: the cited clause answers the question explicitly (fees always payable irrespective of refund), but the row is labeled genuine_ambiguity and the answer manufactures uncertainty
adversarial_metadata.expectation_notes: schema and lints pass; the blind judge should predict clear_answer (label disagreement) and flag overstated ambiguity

messages.content: A customer's UPI payment was authorized late and we never captured it. What happens to the customer's money?
Under Part B, Part I, Clause 3.5, Razorpay will automatically refund the customer, and the customer is guaranteed to receive the money back within 24 hours at no cost to you.

source_clauses.display_citation: Part B SPECIFIC TERMS AND CONDITIONS, Part I Specific Terms For Payment Aggregation Services, Clause 3.5, REFUNDS
source_clauses.relevant_quote: for payments that are late authorized but not captured by You, Razorpay PA may initiate auto-refund to the customer within five (5) days

known_facts.fact: The payment was authorized late and the merchant did not capture it.

adversarial_metadata.planted_flaw: valid citation and quote, but the answer invents a 24-hour guarantee and a fee waiver, and converts the clause's 'may initiate auto-refund within five (5) days' into a certainty
adversarial_metadata.expectation_notes: schema and lints pass; the blind judge should penalize groundedness and no_overreach and flag unsupported_claim/overconfident_answer

messages.content: Razorpay suspended our account under Clause 16. How long can they hold our settlement funds?
Under Part A, Clause 16.1, Razorpay can hold your settlement funds for a maximum of 180 days from the date of suspension, after which the funds must be released to you.

source_clauses.display_citation: Part A GENERAL TERMS AND CONDITIONS, Clause 16.1, SUSPENSION AND TERMINATION
source_clauses.relevant_quote: Razorpay PA shall have the right to immediately suspend Services and settlement of any monies or payments to You, without any liability to You

known_facts.fact: Razorpay suspended the merchant's account under Clause 16.

adversarial_metadata.planted_flaw: mislabeled and overreaching: the ToS is silent on fund-hold duration (this should be genuine_ambiguity), and the answer invents a 180-day maximum that appears nowhere in the cited text
adversarial_metadata.expectation_notes: schema and lints pass; the blind judge should predict genuine_ambiguity (label disagreement) and flag unsupported_claim
